english: to keep inside / to stay indoors
notes: This phrase can mean to keep someone/something inside (transitive) or to stay indoors. It is a combination of the adverb 'drinnen' (inside) and the verb 'halten'. Often used in the context of keeping children or animals indoors, or staying inside due to weather.

sentences.german: Das schlechte Wetter hält uns heute drinnen.
sentences.english: The bad weather is keeping us inside today.
